mnemonics: S.L.T.C. (Supervised Learning for Text Classification): Start with Labeled data, Learn a model, Transform text, Classify new text.
keyPoints: Supervised learning requires a pre-labeled dataset to train a model.
Text must be converted into numerical features (e.g., via TF-IDF or word embeddings) for a machine learning algorithm to process it.
The goal is to build a model that can accurately predict the class label of new, unseen text documents.
commonMistakes: Ignoring the need for text preprocessing (stop word removal, stemming/lemmatization) which can drastically impact model performance.
Treating text as simple 'bag-of-words' and ignoring word order and context, which can be crucial for meaning.
Failing to address class imbalance in the training data, which can lead to a model that is biased towards the majority class.
